original_datasets.name: Semantic verbal fluency responses
original_datasets.columns: [id, vf_an_01, vf_an_02, vf_an_03, vf_an_04, vf_an_05, vf_an_06, vf_an_07, vf_an_08, vf_an_09, vf_an_10, vf_an_11, vf_an_12, vf_an_13, vf_an_14, vf_an_15, vf_an_16, vf_an_17, vf_an_18, vf_an_19, vf_an_20, vf_an_21, vf_an_22, vf_an_23, vf_an_24, vf_an_25, vf_an_26, vf_an_27, vf_an_28, vf_an_29, vf_an_30, vf_an_31, vf_an_32, vf_an_33, vf_an_34, vf_an_35]

summary_statistics.info: 516 rows, 36 columns. id is an identifier; vf_an_* columns contain raw animal fluency responses as strings per position.

original_datasets.notes: Contains raw strings for up to 35 animal responses per participant. Requires cleaning/binarization to a wide binary response matrix for network construction. Does not include the latent openness score; that variable is expected from a separate file.

original_datasets.name: Merged samples with personality measures
original_datasets.notes: Likely contains openness-related variables needed to compute or proxy the latent openness score used to split into High vs Low groups. Column names and exact construction are not stated in the provided materials. Reading .sav requires pyreadstat.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without additional restriction.

analysis_path.path_description: Construct semantic networks for Low vs High openness groups from verbal fluency responses, compute ASPL, CC, Q, and test between-group differences using partial network bootstrapping at 50–90% node retention.
preprocessing_steps: Obtain or merge latent openness score for each participant.
Sort participants by openness and split into equal Low and High halves.
Select response columns (e.g., vf_an_*), clean responses, and binarize to wide matrix.
Remove nodes endorsed by fewer than 2 participants in each group and equate nodes across groups.
Compute cosine similarity between nodes within each group.
Construct sparse networks from similarity matrices.
Compute network measures: ASPL, CC, Q for each group.
Run partial node-retention bootstraps at 0.5, 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9 proportions and summarize group differences.

key_choices.outcome_measure: Average shortest path length (ASPL), clustering coefficient (CC), and modularity (Q) computed on group networks.
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Group membership based on latent openness to experience score (High vs Low; split-by-median/halves).
key_choices.sample_restriction: Split the full sample (N=516) into equal Low (n=258) and High (n=258) openness groups.
key_choices.outlier_rule: Remove nodes (animal responses) endorsed by fewer than 2 participants per group before equating nodes.
key_choices.data_processing: String cleaning and binarization of fluency responses; equate nodes across groups; construct cosine similarity matrices; create sparse graphs; compute network metrics.
key_choices.model_choice: Partial network bootstrapping with t-like summaries of measure differences.

outcome.name: ASPL, CC, Q
outcome.definition: Network measures computed from group semantic networks: average shortest path length (interconnectivity), clustering coefficient (local organization), modularity Q (community structure).

main_predictor.name: openness_group
main_predictor.definition: Binary group indicating High vs Low openness to experience created by splitting the sample into equal halves based on latent openness score.

analysis_code.notes: Script prints a JSON with group measures and bootstrap summaries. Required args include: --task Task1 --data_file /app/data/<file> --id_col id --openness_col <colname> --response_prefix vf_an_. The openness column must be present or merged prior to running.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow this planned path using the original dataset; do not change the focal claim or analysis goal.

tasks.task_instruction: In the partial network that you use, the rate of nodes remaining should be 90%.

analysis_path.path_description: Repeat the semantic network analysis and test group differences using partial node-retention bootstrapping with 90% of nodes retained.
preprocessing_steps: Obtain or merge latent openness score for each participant.
Sort participants by openness and split into equal Low and High halves.
Select response columns (e.g., vf_an_*), clean responses, and binarize to wide matrix.
Remove nodes endorsed by fewer than 2 participants in each group and equate nodes across groups.
Compute cosine similarity between nodes within each group.
Construct sparse networks from similarity matrices.
Compute network measures: ASPL, CC, Q for each group.
Run partial node-retention bootstrap at 0.9 proportion and summarize group differences.

key_choices.outcome_measure: Average shortest path length (ASPL), clustering coefficient (CC), and modularity (Q) computed on group networks.
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Group membership based on latent openness to experience score (High vs Low; split-by-median/halves).
key_choices.sample_restriction: Split the full sample (N=516) into equal Low (n=258) and High (n=258) openness groups.
key_choices.outlier_rule: Remove nodes (animal responses) endorsed by fewer than 2 participants per group before equating nodes.
key_choices.data_processing: String cleaning and binarization of fluency responses; equate nodes across groups; construct cosine similarity matrices; create sparse graphs; compute network metrics.
key_choices.model_choice: Partial network bootstrapping at 90% nodes retained with t-like summaries of measure differences.

outcome.name: ASPL, CC, Q
outcome.definition: Network measures computed from group semantic networks: average shortest path length (interconnectivity), clustering coefficient (local organization), modularity Q (community structure).

main_predictor.name: openness_group
main_predictor.definition: Binary group indicating High vs Low openness to experience created by splitting the sample into equal halves based on latent openness score.

analysis_code.notes: Run with --task Task2 and --bootstrap_prop 0.9. Other required args as in Task1. Openness column must be present or merged prior to running.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the 90% node-retention bootstrap as specified; do not replace with a different test.

files.data/ghz5e_analysis__py.py: Python translation of the R pipeline. Loads dataset(s), splits into High vs Low openness, constructs cosine-similarity graphs, computes ASPL/CC/Q, and runs partial node-retention bootstraps. Prints JSON results to stdout.

codebase.notes: Execution requires specifying id_col, openness_col, and response_prefix (vf_an_). The provided CSV does not include the latent openness column; it must be supplied or merged (e.g., from the .sav). Reading .sav requires pyreadstat. Network modularity uses python-louvain if available.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original dataset(s). Ensure the latent openness score is available or merged into the working dataset, then run the Python entry script to compute group network measures (ASPL, CC, Q) and partial bootstrap summaries.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: For each task, report: (a) High vs Low group measures (ASPL, CC, Q); (b) partial bootstrap t-like statistics and mean differences for ASPL, CC, Q at specified retention levels (50–90% for Task1; 90% for Task2); and (c) conclusion text on whether High shows more interconnectivity (lower ASPL), higher CC, and lower Q than Low.
